# TanStack Router & TanStack Start

TanStack Router is a modern, type-safe router for React and Solid applications that provides end-to-end type safety for routes, params, and loaders. It features schema-driven search params with validation, built-in caching with prefetching and invalidation, nested layouts, transitions, and error boundaries. The router supports both file-based and code-based routing patterns, with automatic route generation and code splitting capabilities.

TanStack Start is a full-stack framework built on TanStack Router, designed for server rendering, streaming, and production-ready deployments. It provides full-document SSR, streaming data, server functions with type-safe RPCs between client and server, middleware support, and universal deployment to any Vite-compatible hosting provider. Start enables building complete full-stack applications while maintaining the excellent developer experience of TanStack Router.

---

## Creating a Router Instance

The `createRouter` function creates the core router instance that manages route matching, navigation, and route transitions. It requires a route tree and supports many configuration options for router-wide settings.

```tsx
import { createRouter, createRootRoute, createRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'

// Define root route
const rootRoute = createRootRoute({
  component: () => (
    <div>
      <nav>
        <Link to="/">Home</Link>
        <Link to="/posts">Posts</Link>
      </nav>
      <Outlet />
    </div>
  ),
  notFoundComponent: () => <div>404 Not Found</div>,
})

// Define child routes
const indexRoute = createRoute({
  getParentRoute: () => rootRoute,
  path: '/',
  component: () => <h1>Welcome Home</h1>,
})

const postsRoute = createRoute({
  getParentRoute: () => rootRoute,
  path: '/posts',
  loader: async () => {
    const res = await fetch('/api/posts')
    return res.json()
  },
  component: PostsComponent,
})

// Build route tree and create router
const routeTree = rootRoute.addChildren([indexRoute, postsRoute])

const router = createRouter({
  routeTree,
  defaultPreload: 'intent',
  defaultStaleTime: 5000,
})

// Register router types for full type safety
declare module '@tanstack/react-router' {
  interface Register {
    router: typeof router
  }
}
```

---

## File-Based Routing with createFileRoute

The `createFileRoute` function creates file-based route instances that are automatically discovered by the TanStack Router CLI. Routes export a `Route` constant and support loaders, components, and all standard route options.

```tsx
// src/routes/posts.$postId.tsx
import { createFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createFileRoute('/posts/$postId')({
  // Loader runs before component renders, data is cached
  loader: async ({ params }) => {
    const res = await fetch(`/api/posts/${params.postId}`)
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Post not found')
    return res.json()
  },
  // Pending component shows while loader is running (after 1s by default)
  pendingComponent: () => <div>Loading post...</div>,
  // Error component renders if loader throws
  errorComponent: ({ error }) => <div>Error: {error.message}</div>,
  // Main component receives loader data via hook
  component: PostComponent,
})

function PostComponent() {
  const post = Route.useLoaderData()
  const { postId } = Route.useParams()

  return (
    <article>
      <h1>{post.title}</h1>
      <p>Post ID: {postId}</p>
      <div>{post.content}</div>
    </article>
  )
}
```

---

## Link Component for Navigation

The `Link` component renders type-safe anchor tags with proper `href` attributes, supporting cmd/ctrl+click, preloading on hover, and active state styling.

```tsx
import { Link } from '@tanstack/react-router'

function Navigation() {
  return (
    <nav>
      {/* Basic link */}
      <Link to="/about">About</Link>

      {/* Link with path params */}
      <Link to="/posts/$postId" params={{ postId: '123' }}>
        View Post
      </Link>

      {/* Link with search params */}
      <Link to="/search" search={{ query: 'tanstack', page: 1 }}>
        Search
      </Link>

      {/* Link with search param updater function */}
      <Link to="." search={(prev) => ({ ...prev, page: (prev.page || 1) + 1 })}>
        Next Page
      </Link>

      {/* Active styling */}
      <Link
        to="/dashboard"
        activeProps={{ className: 'font-bold text-blue-500' }}
        inactiveProps={{ className: 'text-gray-500' }}
      >
        Dashboard
      </Link>

      {/* Preload on hover */}
      <Link to="/expensive-page" preload="intent" preloadDelay={100}>
        Expensive Page
      </Link>

      {/* Relative navigation */}
      <Link from="/posts/$postId" to="..">
        Back to Posts
      </Link>
    </nav>
  )
}
```

---

## useNavigate Hook for Programmatic Navigation

The `useNavigate` hook returns a function for imperative navigation, useful for side effects like form submissions or successful async actions.

```tsx
import { useNavigate } from '@tanstack/react-router'

function CreatePostForm() {
  const navigate = useNavigate()

  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => {
    e.preventDefault()
    const formData = new FormData(e.currentTarget)

    const response = await fetch('/api/posts', {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: formData.get('title') }),
    })

    if (response.ok) {
      const { id } = await response.json()
      // Navigate to new post after creation
      navigate({
        to: '/posts/$postId',
        params: { postId: id },
        replace: true, // Replace current history entry
      })
    }
  }

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <input name="title" placeholder="Post title" />
      <button type="submit">Create Post</button>
    </form>
  )
}

// Navigate with search params
function SearchForm() {
  const navigate = useNavigate({ from: '/search' })

  const handleSearch = (query: string) => {
    navigate({
      search: (prev) => ({ ...prev, query, page: 1 }),
    })
  }

  return <input onChange={(e) => handleSearch(e.target.value)} />
}
```

---

## Data Loading with Route Loaders

Route loaders fetch data before rendering, with built-in SWR caching, stale-while-revalidate patterns, and dependency-based cache invalidation.

```tsx
// src/routes/posts.tsx
import { createFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createFileRoute('/posts')({
  // Define which search params affect the loader cache key
  loaderDeps: ({ search: { page, limit } }) => ({ page, limit }),

  // Loader receives params, deps, context, and abort signal
  loader: async ({ deps: { page, limit }, abortController }) => {
    const res = await fetch(`/api/posts?page=${page}&limit=${limit}`, {
      signal: abortController.signal,
    })
    return res.json()
  },

  // Control cache freshness (default: 0ms, always revalidate)
  staleTime: 10_000, // 10 seconds

  // Control how long data stays in cache after unmount
  gcTime: 300_000, // 5 minutes

  component: PostsComponent,
})

function PostsComponent() {
  const { posts, totalPages } = Route.useLoaderData()
  const { page } = Route.useSearch()

  return (
    <div>
      {posts.map((post) => (
        <div key={post.id}>{post.title}</div>
      ))}
      <Link search={(prev) => ({ ...prev, page: page + 1 })}>
        Next Page
      </Link>
    </div>
  )
}
```

---

## Search Params Validation with Zod

TanStack Router provides JSON-first search params with full type safety through validation schemas using Zod, Valibot, or ArkType.

```tsx
// src/routes/shop.products.tsx
import { createFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { z } from 'zod'
import { zodValidator, fallback } from '@tanstack/zod-adapter'

const productSearchSchema = z.object({
  page: fallback(z.number(), 1).default(1),
  sort: fallback(z.enum(['newest', 'oldest', 'price']), 'newest').default('newest'),
  category: z.string().optional(),
  minPrice: z.number().optional(),
  maxPrice: z.number().optional(),
})

export const Route = createFileRoute('/shop/products')({
  // Validate and type search params
  validateSearch: zodValidator(productSearchSchema),

  // Use validated search params in loader
  loaderDeps: ({ search }) => ({
    page: search.page,
    sort: search.sort,
    category: search.category,
  }),

  loader: async ({ deps }) => fetchProducts(deps),

  component: ProductsComponent,
})

function ProductsComponent() {
  const { page, sort, category } = Route.useSearch()

  return (
    <div>
      <select
        value={sort}
        onChange={(e) => (
          <Link search={(prev) => ({ ...prev, sort: e.target.value })} />
        )}
      >
        <option value="newest">Newest</option>
        <option value="oldest">Oldest</option>
        <option value="price">Price</option>
      </select>

      <Link search={(prev) => ({ ...prev, page: page + 1 })}>
        Next Page
      </Link>
    </div>
  )
}
```

---

## useSearch Hook for Reading Search Params

The `useSearch` hook provides type-safe access to validated search parameters from anywhere in the component tree.

```tsx
import { useSearch, getRouteApi } from '@tanstack/react-router'

// Option 1: Use from route directly
function ProductFilters() {
  const { sort, category, minPrice, maxPrice } = useSearch({
    from: '/shop/products',
  })

  return (
    <div>
      <p>Current sort: {sort}</p>
      <p>Category: {category ?? 'All'}</p>
      <p>Price range: {minPrice ?? 0} - {maxPrice ?? 'unlimited'}</p>
    </div>
  )
}

// Option 2: Use getRouteApi to avoid circular imports
const routeApi = getRouteApi('/shop/products')

function ProductSidebar() {
  const search = routeApi.useSearch()
  const params = routeApi.useParams()
  const loaderData = routeApi.useLoaderData()

  return <div>...</div>
}

// Option 3: Loose search (from any route, partial types)
function GlobalSearchIndicator() {
  const search = useSearch({ strict: false })
  // search is Partial<AllSearchParams>

  return search.query ? <span>Searching: {search.query}</span> : null
}
```

---

## useParams Hook for Path Parameters

The `useParams` hook returns all parsed path parameters from the current route and its parents with full type safety.

```tsx
import { useParams, getRouteApi } from '@tanstack/react-router'

// Route: /posts/$postId/comments/$commentId
function CommentComponent() {
  // Full type safety when specifying route
  const { postId, commentId } = useParams({ from: '/posts/$postId/comments/$commentId' })

  return (
    <div>
      <p>Post: {postId}</p>
      <p>Comment: {commentId}</p>
    </div>
  )
}

// Using getRouteApi for code-split components
const routeApi = getRouteApi('/posts/$postId')

function PostHeader() {
  const { postId } = routeApi.useParams()

  return <h1>Post #{postId}</h1>
}

// Select specific param with transformation
function PostIdDisplay() {
  const numericId = useParams({
    from: '/posts/$postId',
    select: (params) => parseInt(params.postId, 10),
  })

  return <span>Numeric ID: {numericId}</span>
}
```

---

## useLoaderData Hook for Accessing Loader Data

The `useLoaderData` hook retrieves data returned by the route's loader function with full type inference.

```tsx
import { useLoaderData, getRouteApi } from '@tanstack/react-router'

// Direct usage with from option
function PostContent() {
  const post = useLoaderData({ from: '/posts/$postId' })
  // post is fully typed based on loader return type

  return (
    <article>
      <h1>{post.title}</h1>
      <p>{post.body}</p>
      <span>By {post.author.name}</span>
    </article>
  )
}

// Using Route object directly (in same file as route definition)
export const Route = createFileRoute('/posts/$postId')({
  loader: async ({ params }) => {
    const post = await fetchPost(params.postId)
    return { post, comments: await fetchComments(params.postId) }
  },
  component: PostPage,
})

function PostPage() {
  const { post, comments } = Route.useLoaderData()

  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{post.title}</h1>
      <ul>
        {comments.map((c) => (
          <li key={c.id}>{c.text}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  )
}

// Select specific data to minimize re-renders
function CommentCount() {
  const count = useLoaderData({
    from: '/posts/$postId',
    select: (data) => data.comments.length,
  })

  return <span>{count} comments</span>
}
```

---

## useBlocker Hook for Navigation Blocking

The `useBlocker` hook prevents navigation when certain conditions are met, such as unsaved form changes.

```tsx
import { useBlocker } from '@tanstack/react-router'
import { useState } from 'react'

function EditPostForm() {
  const [isDirty, setIsDirty] = useState(false)

  // Simple blocking without custom UI
  useBlocker({
    shouldBlockFn: () => isDirty,
    enableBeforeUnload: true, // Also block browser refresh/close
  })

  return (
    <form onChange={() => setIsDirty(true)}>
      <textarea name="content" />
      <button type="submit">Save</button>
    </form>
  )
}

// With custom confirmation UI
function EditPostFormWithDialog() {
  const [isDirty, setIsDirty] = useState(false)

  const { proceed, reset, status, next } = useBlocker({
    shouldBlockFn: () => isDirty,
    withResolver: true,
  })

  return (
    <>
      <form onChange={() => setIsDirty(true)}>
        <textarea name="content" />
        <button type="submit">Save</button>
      </form>

      {status === 'blocked' && (
        <dialog open>
          <p>You have unsaved changes!</p>
          <p>Navigating to: {next.pathname}</p>
          <button onClick={proceed}>Leave anyway</button>
          <button onClick={reset}>Stay on page</button>
        </dialog>
      )}
    </>
  )
}
```

---

## useRouter Hook for Router Instance Access

The `useRouter` hook returns the router instance for accessing router methods and state imperatively.

```tsx
import { useRouter } from '@tanstack/react-router'

function RefreshButton() {
  const router = useRouter()

  const handleRefresh = () => {
    // Invalidate all routes and refetch
    router.invalidate()
  }

  const handleNavigate = () => {
    // Programmatic navigation via router
    router.navigate({
      to: '/posts',
      search: { page: 1 },
    })
  }

  return (
    <div>
      <button onClick={handleRefresh}>Refresh Data</button>
      <button onClick={handleNavigate}>Go to Posts</button>
    </div>
  )
}

// Access router state (non-reactive, use useRouterState for reactive)
function RouterInfo() {
  const router = useRouter()

  // Current location info
  console.log(router.state.location.pathname)
  console.log(router.state.location.search)

  // Preload a route
  const handlePreload = () => {
    router.preloadRoute({ to: '/expensive-page' })
  }

  return <button onMouseEnter={handlePreload}>Hover to preload</button>
}
```

---

## Outlet Component for Nested Routes

The `Outlet` component renders child route components, enabling nested layouts and route hierarchies.

```tsx
import { createRootRoute, createRoute, Outlet, Link } from '@tanstack/react-router'

// Root layout with outlet
const rootRoute = createRootRoute({
  component: () => (
    <div className="app">
      <header>
        <nav>
          <Link to="/">Home</Link>
          <Link to="/dashboard">Dashboard</Link>
        </nav>
      </header>
      <main>
        <Outlet /> {/* Child routes render here */}
      </main>
      <footer>Copyright 2024</footer>
    </div>
  ),
})

// Dashboard layout with nested outlet
const dashboardRoute = createRoute({
  getParentRoute: () => rootRoute,
  path: '/dashboard',
  component: () => (
    <div className="dashboard">
      <aside>
        <Link to="/dashboard/analytics">Analytics</Link>
        <Link to="/dashboard/settings">Settings</Link>
      </aside>
      <section>
        <Outlet /> {/* Nested dashboard routes render here */}
      </section>
    </div>
  ),
})

// Nested route under dashboard
const analyticsRoute = createRoute({
  getParentRoute: () => dashboardRoute,
  path: '/analytics',
  component: () => <h1>Analytics Dashboard</h1>,
})
```

---

## Server Functions with createServerFn (TanStack Start)

Server functions enable type-safe RPCs between client and server, running exclusively on the server while callable from anywhere in your application.

```tsx
// src/utils/posts.functions.ts
import { createServerFn } from '@tanstack/react-start'
import { z } from 'zod'

// Simple server function
export const getServerTime = createServerFn().handler(async () => {
  return new Date().toISOString()
})

// Server function with validation
const CreatePostSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string().min(1),
  content: z.string().min(10),
})

export const createPost = createServerFn({ method: 'POST' })
  .inputValidator(CreatePostSchema)
  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
    // Access database, environment variables, etc.
    const post = await db.posts.create({
      data: { title: data.title, content: data.content },
    })
    return post
  })

// Server function with redirect
export const requireAuth = createServerFn().handler(async () => {
  const user = await getCurrentUser()

  if (!user) {
    throw redirect({ to: '/login' })
  }

  return user
})

// Usage in route loader
export const Route = createFileRoute('/posts')({
  loader: () => getPosts(),
})

// Usage in component
function CreatePostForm() {
  const handleSubmit = async (formData: FormData) => {
    const post = await createPost({
      data: {
        title: formData.get('title') as string,
        content: formData.get('content') as string,
      },
    })
    console.log('Created:', post)
  }
}
```

---

## Server Context and Request Handling (TanStack Start)

TanStack Start provides utilities to access request information and customize responses within server functions.

```tsx
import { createServerFn } from '@tanstack/react-start'
import {
  getRequest,
  getRequestHeader,
  setResponseHeaders,
  setResponseStatus,
} from '@tanstack/react-start/server'

export const getCachedData = createServerFn({ method: 'GET' }).handler(
  async () => {
    // Access the incoming request
    const request = getRequest()
    console.log('Request URL:', request.url)

    // Read specific headers
    const authHeader = getRequestHeader('Authorization')
    const userAgent = getRequestHeader('User-Agent')

    // Set response caching headers
    setResponseHeaders(
      new Headers({
        'Cache-Control': 'public, max-age=300',
        'CDN-Cache-Control': 'max-age=3600, stale-while-revalidate=600',
      }),
    )

    // Set custom status code
    setResponseStatus(200)

    // Verify authentication
    if (!authHeader) {
      setResponseStatus(401)
      throw new Error('Unauthorized')
    }

    return { data: 'cached response', timestamp: Date.now() }
  },
)

// Form data handling
export const submitContactForm = createServerFn({ method: 'POST' })
  .inputValidator((data) => {
    if (!(data instanceof FormData)) {
      throw new Error('Expected FormData')
    }
    return {
      name: data.get('name')?.toString() || '',
      email: data.get('email')?.toString() || '',
      message: data.get('message')?.toString() || '',
    }
  })
  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
    await sendEmail(data.email, data.name, data.message)
    return { success: true }
  })
```

---

## Code Splitting with Lazy Routes

TanStack Router supports automatic and manual code splitting to reduce bundle sizes and improve load performance.

```tsx
// Automatic code splitting (vite.config.ts)
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import { tanstackRouter } from '@tanstack/router-plugin/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    tanstackRouter({
      autoCodeSplitting: true, // Automatically splits components
    }),
    react(),
  ],
})

// Manual code splitting with .lazy.tsx files
// src/routes/posts.tsx (critical config)
import { createFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createFileRoute('/posts')({
  loader: () => fetchPosts(), // Loaders stay in main bundle
})

// src/routes/posts.lazy.tsx (lazy-loaded component)
import { createLazyFileRoute } from '@tanstack/react-router'

export const Route = createLazyFileRoute('/posts')({
  component: PostsComponent,
  pendingComponent: () => <div>Loading...</div>,
  errorComponent: ({ error }) => <div>Error: {error.message}</div>,
})

function PostsComponent() {
  const posts = Route.useLoaderData()
  return <div>{posts.map((p) => <PostCard key={p.id} post={p} />)}</div>
}

// Code-based lazy loading
const postsRoute = createRoute({
  getParentRoute: () => rootRoute,
  path: '/posts',
}).lazy(() => import('./posts.lazy').then((d) => d.Route))
```

---

## Summary

TanStack Router and TanStack Start provide a comprehensive solution for building type-safe, performant web applications. The router excels at client-side routing with features like nested layouts, type-safe navigation, validated search params, and built-in data loading with SWR caching. Its file-based routing system automatically generates route trees while maintaining full TypeScript support throughout the navigation stack.

TanStack Start extends these capabilities to full-stack development, enabling server-side rendering, streaming, and type-safe server functions that bridge the client-server boundary seamlessly. Common integration patterns include combining with TanStack Query for advanced data fetching, using Zod or Valibot for search param validation, implementing authentication flows with route guards and redirects, and leveraging automatic code splitting for optimal bundle sizes. The framework's deployment-agnostic architecture supports any Vite-compatible hosting platform, from serverless functions to traditional Node.js servers.
